angband_n.d: Morgoth's realm of the North; Thangorodrim above the gate; hosts bred beneath.

angband_x.d: 'The borders of Dor-deloth ever increased southward': Dorthonion, the Pass, Hithlum's fences taken.
angband_x.c: Silm 18–20; HoME V List of Names

hithlum.d: Walled by Ered Wethrin and Ered Lómin; Mithrim and Dor-lómin within; Hador's Men federate.

gondolin.d: The Hidden City in the Echoriath ring; one army of 10,000.

dorthonion.d: The pine highland, sixty leagues west to east; lost in the Bragollach.

doriath.d: The Girdle of Melian about Neldoreth, Region and Nivrim; Menegroth its heart.

nargothrond.d: From Sirion west to Nenning and the sea; the Guarded Plain watched from the caves.

falas.d: The walled havens Brithombar and Eglarest and the western shore.

brethil.d: The forest between Teiglin and Sirion, held by Haleth's folk under Doriath's leave.

marches.n: The March of Maedhros & the East
marches.d: Himring, Aglon and Maglor's Gap; Himlad; Thargelion under Caranthir (to 455).

ossiriand.d: The Land of Seven Rivers between Gelion and the mountains; bowmen who refuse open war.

lindon.d: Ossiriand's remnant about the Gulf of Lhûn; the last high-elven kingdom, then Círdan's havens.

eregion.d: The Ring-smiths' land of holly; south border the Glanduin; destroyed by Sauron.

khazad.d: Greatest of the mansions of the Dwarves; gates shut through the war of 1697.

lorinand.d: The golden wood between Celebrant and Anduin; Amdír, Amroth, then Galadriel.

greenwood.d: All the forest under the Silvan king before the Shadow.

numdom.d: Lond Daer (Aldarion), Pelargir (2350, the Faithful), Umbar (2280, the King's Men).

mordor.d: The fenced Black Land: Ephel Dúath, Ered Lithui, Barad-dûr; Núrn's slave-fields.

mordor_e.d: Emptied by the Last Alliance; Gondor's fortresses watch the passes until the Great Plague.

arnor.d: 'All Eriador except the regions beyond the Lune and the lands east of Greyflood and Loudwater.'

arthedain.d: 'Between Brandywine and Lune, and the land north of the Great Road as far as the Weather Hills.'

cardolan.d: 'Between the Brandywine, the Greyflood, and the Great Road'; wasted by 1409 and the Plague.

rhudaur.d: 'Between the Ettenmoors, the Weather Hills, and the Misty Mountains, with the Angle'; fell to Angmar.

angmar.d: 'Lands on both sides of the Mountains'; seat at Carn Dûm; broken at Fornost.

gondor_pk.d: 'North to Celebrant and the eaves of Mirkwood; west to Greyflood; east to Rhûn; south to Harnen and Umbar.' Mordor desolate within, under watch.

gondor_lt.d: Anórien and the southern fiefs; Ithilien contested; Calenardhon ceded 2510.

rohan.d: Cirion's grant: Anduin to Isen, Limlight to the mountains; Isengard a Gondorian enclave.

shire.d: Grant of Argeleb II: Baranduin to the Far Downs; Buckland 2340; Westmarch S.R. 1452.

bree.d: Four villages at the road-crossing; oldest settlement of Men and hobbits together.

rivendell.d: The hidden refuge of the Bruinen valley.

thranduil.d: Withdrawn north of the Mountains of Mirkwood before the Shadow; halls on the Forest River.

dolguldur.d: The Shadow's hill-fortress in the south forest; razed by Galadriel after the War.

dale.d: The Mountain-kingdom and the town of the bowmen; ruined by Smaug, restored after Five Armies.

ironhills.d: Dáin's realm; the mailed veterans of the East.

isengard_s.d: The Wizard's Vale and ring of stone; unmade by the Ents into the Treegarth.

umbar.d: The rebel haven of the King's Men's heirs; fifty great dromunds at the Pelennor.

harad.d: The wide South of the Haradrim; mûmakil war-hosts. Bounds unrecorded.

rhun.d: The Easterling steppes about the inland Sea. Bounds unrecorded.

khand.d: Land of the Variags, south-east of Mordor. Bounds unrecorded.

reunited.d: Arnor and Gondor under one crown; the Shire a protected Free Land; Ithilien under Faramir.

eastlorien.d: 'The forest south of the Narrows' given to Celeborn at the partition of Eryn Lasgalen.

slices.label: Years of the Trees
ev: The Girdle of Melian fenced about Doriath
Nogrod and Belegost delved in Ered Luin; Khazad-dûm far east
Angband rebuilt upon Utumno's western outworks
World-shape: the marred symmetry after the Lamps; schematic only

slices.label: F.A. 65–455 — the Long Peace
ev: The Noldor's leaguer rings Angband; realms as charted in Silm 14
Gondolin full-wrought after 52 years of secret toil
The Edain enter Beleriand c. 310–330 and hold fiefs under the Elf-kings

slices.label: F.A. 455–583 — the Ruin of Beleriand
ev: Ard-galen burned: Anfauglith, the Gasping Dust
Nirnaeth 472; Falas 473; Nargothrond 495; Doriath c.502; Gondolin 510

slices.label: F.A. 587 / S.A. 1 — After the War of Wrath
ev: 'Northward and westward many lands sank'; the Gulf of Lhûn breaks Ered Luin
Lindon remains of Ossiriand; Himling and Tol Fuin stand above the waves
S.A. 32: Númenor raised for the Edain — far west, off this canvas

slices.label: S.A. 750–1690 — Númenor's Noontide
ev: Eregion founded 750; the Rings forged c.1500–90
Vinyalondë/Lond Daer: the great deforestation of Minhiriath & Enedwaith
c.1000: Sauron takes Mordor; Barad-dûr rises

slices.label: S.A. 1693–3319 — War and Downfall
slices.sub: to the Change of the World
ev: 1697: Eregion destroyed; Imladris founded; Moria's gates shut
3319 the Downfall: world made round; Bay of Belfalas filled at east and south; Pelargir left far inland; Tolfalas nearly destroyed

slices.label: S.A. 3320 – T.A. 2 — Realms in Exile
ev: Arnor: 'all Eriador except beyond the Lune and east of Greyflood'
Gondor about the lower Anduin: Osgiliath, Minas Ithil, Minas Anor
3429–41: Last Alliance; Mordor emptied for two millennia

slices.label: T.A. 861–1300 — Division & Gondor's Height
ev: 861: Arnor divided — Arthedain, Cardolan, Rhudaur; Amon Sûl the tri-point
c.1050: the Shadow in Greenwood; men begin to call it Mirkwood
1601: the Shire granted by Argeleb II (late in this slice)

slices.label: T.A. 1300–1975 — the Angmar Wars
slices.sub: plague and the fall of the North
ev: c.1300: the Witch-realm at the Mountains' head; 1409 Amon Sûl razed; Rhudaur absorbed
1636: the Great Plague — Cardolan dies, Osgiliath depopulated, the watch on Mordor ceases
1974–75: Arthedain falls; Angmar broken at Fornost; the North Kingdom ends

slices.label: T.A. 1980–2510 — the Wandering Days
ev: 1980–81: the Balrog; Khazad-dûm abandoned; Lórien's flight begins
2002: Minas Ithil taken — Minas Morgul; 2050 the kings end
1999 Erebor founded; 2510 Field of Celebrant — Calenardhon granted to Eorl

slices.label: T.A. 2510–2941 — Rohan and the Dragon
ev: 2510: the Oath of Cirion and Eorl; Isengard kept by Gondor (Saruman from 2759)
2770: Smaug — the Desolation about Erebor and Dale (healed after 2941)
2912: floods ruin Tharbad; 2941: Erebor restored, the Necromancer driven out

slices.label: T.A. 2951–3019 — the War of the Ring
ev: 2951: Sauron declares himself; Barad-dûr rebuilt; Mount Doom rekindled
3018–19: the fronts — Isen, Hornburg, Pelennor, Morannon, Dale, Lórien ×3, Mirkwood
3019: Barad-dûr and the Towers fall in ruin; Isengard becomes the Treegarth; Núrn freed

slices.label: The Fourth Age — the Reunited Kingdom
ev: Arnor and Gondor reunited under Elessar; Annúminas restored
Eryn Lasgalen partitioned: Thranduil north, East Lórien south, Woodmen between
Minas Morgul destroyed by decree; Ithilien the garden of the realm again
The Elves diminish and depart; the mallorn blooms in the Party Field
